_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: FL local batch (5) — Miami D1/D3/D4 commissioners + Orlando Mayor Dyer + Orlando D4 Sheehan. Rosters web-verified June 2026 against miami.gov / orlando.gov. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable source; null where no evidence (no party-default inference). Public_justice q0 held to the AFFIRMATIVE-advocacy bar per skill discipline (mere budget-funding-the-police = null; documented public statement / program = TRUE). Refuse_state_overreach q0 held strict: rubric specifies 'UNCONSTITUTIONAL state mandates' — Dyer's / Sheehan's progressive resistance to anti-sanctuary + anti-Pride state directives does NOT clear the bar (state directives are constitutional; resistance is in opposite cultural direction) → NULL. Refuse_state_overreach q3 (subsidiarity) is content-neutral and IS scored TRUE for Sheehan on her explicit 'lowest-level action' framing. ESCALONA CORRECTION: original prompt premise (Carollo-aide, appointed early 2025) was WRONG — he is a political newcomer (Sexy Fish GM + real-estate broker) who won the Dec 9 2025 runoff vs Frank Carollo (53%), sworn in Dec 17 2025. ESCALONA R-DEVIATION FLAG: publicly stated he 'would consider cancelling Miami's 287(g) ICE agreement if another commissioner proposed it' (Miami Herald via NewTimes + AOL/Herald op-ed) → border_immigration.2 FALSE despite R label. ORLANDO ECON Q2 ASYMMETRY: Dyer scored TRUE (12-yr flat millage CFPublic-cited as restraint); Sheehan scored NULL (agent's 2015 millage-hike claim lacked verifiable URL — only the same flat-millage CFPublic article was substantively citable). KNOWN SLUG-COLLISION DEFERRALS UNCHANGED: rick-west (VA Chesapeake Mayor + OK State Rep) and andre-dickens (FL ghost IDX 7663 + GA evidence_local Atlanta Mayor IDX 8955) still flagged for manual dedup-merge — NOT touched this batch.

set.office: City Commissioner, District 1, City of Miami, Florida (sworn in Dec 2, 2023 · term to Nov 2027 · 2023 runoff winner ~54% vs Alex Diaz de la Portilla)

profile.background: Miguel Angel Gabela — Cuban-born (immigrated to U.S. at age 6, 1970). Auto-parts business owner ~40 years in District 1 with real-estate investments. Married to Mariela for 36 years; two daughters, one grandchild. Prior service on City of Miami Zoning Board. Lost 2019 D1 race, won 2023 D1 runoff against Alex Diaz de la Portilla (~54%). District covers Flagami, Allapattah, parts of Little Havana. One of two NO votes on the Commission's attempted ordinance to push elections from odd to even years (extending sitting terms ~1 yr); appeals court later struck the ordinance down as unconstitutional.

miguel-angel-gabela.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 1 Commissioner of Miami as of June 2026 (miami.gov bio + Floridian Press Feb 2026 + Miami Today Dec 2025 coverage). Sweep of miamigov.com, Miami Herald / New Times, NBC6/WPLG/WSVN, WLRN, Floridian Press, his campaign site (gabela4miami.com), Ballotpedia. HARD evidence cited: economic_stewardship q2 TRUE — Gabela + Pardo pushed $25M tax cut Dec 2023 after state ruled original budget illegal, plus proposed suspending recycling to save $2.5M in FY25-26 (affirmative restraint posture beyond routine vote). public_justice q0 TRUE — campaign 5-point plan explicitly called for 'additional police staff, enhanced bike and foot patrols' + 'stronger police presence' at Sewell Park (affirmative advocacy, not just budget). border_immigration q2 TRUE — voted YES June 2025 on Miami 287(g) ICE deputization agreement (3-2). refuse_state_overreach q0 FALSE — justified the 287(g) vote as 'We signed this to stay within the law in Florida, ensuring we don't lose critical funds' (Cuba Headlines), explicit COMPLIANCE-with-state-funding rationale not local-control resistance. All other 26 cells null after exhaustive sweep — no public statements located on life, marriage, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, broader public-justice cells (rogue prosecutors / victim restitution / 'public health' framing / Rom 13), subsidiarity, or 2A. NO Pride proclamation / drag-library / CRT vote record at city level. NOT-SCORED note: agent suggested refuse_state_overreach q4 'sideways TRUE' for his NO vote against the unconstitutional term-extension city ord — held NULL because that was refusing a CITY overreach not a STATE directive (strict rubric reading).

2.note: Dec 2023 co-pushed $25M tax cut after state ruled original Miami budget illegal; FY25-26 also proposed suspending recycling to save $2.5M — affirmative fiscal-restraint posture beyond routine budget votes

0.note: Campaign 5-point plan called for 'additional police staff, enhanced bike and foot patrols' + monthly neighborhood safety meetings + 'stronger police presence' at Sewell Park — affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond a budget vote

On the June 2025 287(g) ICE vote: 'We signed this to stay within the law in Florida, ensuring we don't lose critical funds' — explicitly framed compliance with state mandate to preserve $20-30M state funding, opposite of local-control resistance

2.note: Voted YES June 2025 to enter Miami 287(g) ICE deputization (3-2) — affirmatively integrates city police with federal immigration enforcement, opposite of sanctuary policy

set.office: City Commissioner, District 4, City of Miami, Florida (sworn in June 10, 2025 · special election to fill late Comm Manolo Reyes seat · term through late 2027)

profile.religion: No public faith profession located (Catholic-coded via Corpus Christi PACT event attendance but no profession)
profile.background: Ralph Rafael Rosado, age 52 — Miami native, son of Cuban exiles. PhD City Planning, University of Pennsylvania; joint Master's (Public Policy + Urban Planning), Princeton; BA English, FIU Honors College. President of Rosado and Associates (urban planning / economic development); Senior Fellow, FIU Metropolitan Center; instructor at UM and FIU. Prior public service: Village Manager, North Bay Village (2019-2024); chaired Miami's $400M Miami Forever Bond Oversight Board; founding President, Abuelos Foundation. Won June 3, 2025 Miami D4 special election with 55.14% against Jose Regalado to fill late Commissioner Manolo Reyes's seat; sworn in June 10, 2025. Endorsed by Republican Liberty Caucus of Miami-Dade, Miami FOP Lodge #20, AFSCME Local 1907, Venezuelan American Republican Club. Backed by Comm. Joe Carollo and Mayor Francis Suarez. Now chairs Downtown Development Authority. Nonpartisan office; party listed R.

ralph-rafael-rosado.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 4 Commissioner of Miami as of June 2026 (miami.gov D4 bio + May 2026 Rosado Scholars resolution + Jan 2026 Venezuela presser). Sweep of miamigov.com, Miami Herald / New Times, CBS Miami, WLRN, Florida Politics, his campaign trail coverage, FIU SIPA bio, FOP endorsement, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence cited: economic_stewardship q2 TRUE — spearheaded FY25-26 100% city-portion property-tax rebate for homesteaded seniors 65+; on record: 'City of Miami is in a strong financial position — maintaining low millage rates, healthy reserves, and a balanced surplus' (Miami Today Aug 2025). public_justice q0 TRUE — Miami FOP Lodge #20 endorsement explicitly cited 'longstanding commitment to public safety' + campaign pledge to 'increase resources for police' (affirmative advocacy beyond budget). border_immigration q2 TRUE — voted YES June 18 2025 on 287(g) ICE deputization, said Miami 'should be the model... other cities should follow our lead.' All other 27 cells null after sweep — no public statements located on life, marriage, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, broader public-justice cells, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A. RLC endorsement frames him as 'limited government, fiscal responsibility, individual liberty' but caucus vetting doesn't substitute for his own statements per skill discipline — coverage gap typical for first-term freshman commissioner whose portfolio is zoning/budget/services.

2.note: Spearheaded FY25-26 100% city-portion property-tax rebate for homesteaded seniors 65+; on-record 'City of Miami is in a strong financial position — maintaining low millage rates, healthy reserves, and a balanced surplus' — affirmative tax-relief / restraint posture

0.note: Miami FOP Lodge #20 endorsement cited 'longstanding commitment to public safety'; campaign pledge to 'increase resources for police' — affirmative back-the-blue posture beyond budget vote

2.note: Voted YES June 18 2025 on Miami 287(g) ICE deputization agreement; said Miami 'should be the model... other cities should follow our lead' — affirmative anti-sanctuary / pro-federal-preemption

set.office: City Commissioner, District 3, City of Miami, Florida (sworn in Dec 17, 2025 · 4-yr term to 2029 · won Dec 9, 2025 runoff ~53% vs Frank Carollo)

profile.background: Rolando Escalona — born in Havana, Cuba; immigrated to Miami ~2014. Restaurant GM at Brickell's Sexy Fish (157 employees); licensed real-estate broker. Education: Miami Dade College → Florida International University (political science / international relations). Married, twin children. First elected office. Won Dec 9, 2025 D3 runoff with ~53% against Frank Carollo (brother of predecessor Joe Carollo) — political-newcomer outsider beating the Carollo dynasty for the seat. Sworn in Dec 17, 2025; 4-year term through 2029. Endorsed by Veterans For America First (VFAF); also endorsed by his prior-round opponents Alejandro + Piper before the runoff. District 3 covers Little Havana, East Shenandoah, West Brickell, parts of Silver Bluff/The Roads. Hired Ariel Trueba (former 2-term chair of Miami-Dade LGBTQ+ Advisory Board) as staff — signals openness, not an affirmative trans/LGBTQ statement either way.

rolando-escalona.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 3 Commissioner of Miami as of June 2026 (miami.gov D3 bio + CBS swearing-in + Political Cortadito Dec 18 2025). CORRECTION TO PRIOR PROMPT PREMISE: Escalona was NOT a Carollo aide and was NOT appointed in early 2025 — he is a political newcomer (Sexy Fish GM + real-estate broker) who beat Frank Carollo in the Dec 9 2025 runoff. Sweep of miamigov.com, Miami Herald / New Times, NBC6, CBS Miami, WLRN, Florida Politics, Political Cortadito, Calle Ocho News, Miami Today, AOL/Herald op-ed, VFAF endorsement, Hy-Lo News. HARD evidence cited: public_justice q0 TRUE — co-sponsored $450M 'Safe and Ready Miami' public-safety bond going to voters Aug 2026 with on-record statement 'This bond is an investment that reflects my commitment to making public safety a top priority'; campaign promise to hire more officers + restore street patrols (affirmative advocacy bar cleared). border_immigration q2 FALSE — R-DEVIATION FLAG — per Miami Herald (cited in New Times Aug 2026 op-ed + AOL/Herald op-ed) Escalona publicly stated he 'would consider cancelling the city's 287(g) ICE agreement if another commissioner proposed it,' openness to anti-cooperation contrary to sanctuary-opposition cell. All other 28 cells null after sweep — no statements located on life, marriage (no Pride/drag vote on record; 'Little Havana Pride' is neighborhood-pride NOT LGBT Pride per WLRN), family/schools (out of city-comm jurisdiction), Christian liberty, election integrity, broader public-justice cells, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A (FL constitutional carry is state law, no Miami gun-ordinance vote on record). economic_stewardship q2 held NULL — $450M Safe & Ready bond IS deficit financing but campaigned 'lower taxes for seniors'; mixed signal pending a millage vote. Hire of LGBTQ+ Advisory Board chair Trueba as staff signals openness but is not a vote → biblical_marriage cells stay null per no-overread rule.

0.note: Co-sponsored $450M 'Safe and Ready Miami' public-safety bond (Aug 2026 ballot) explicitly to fund police/fire stations; on-record 'This bond is an investment that reflects my commitment to making public safety a top priority'; campaign promise to hire more officers + restore street patrols — affirmative pro-police advocacy

2.note: Per Miami Herald (cited in NewTimes op-ed + AOL/Herald op-ed), Escalona publicly stated he 'would consider cancelling the city's 287(g) ICE agreement if another commissioner proposed it' — openness to anti-ICE-cooperation is R-DEVIATION contrary to sanctuary-opposition rubric

set.office: Mayor of Orlando, Florida (sitting since March 2003 · longest-serving mayor in city history · re-elected Nov 2023 with 72.8% for 7th term · stated final term · term to Jan 10, 2028 · 2027 race is open seat)

profile.background: John Hugh 'Buddy' Dyer (b. Aug 7, 1958, Kissimmee FL). BA civil engineering Brown University; JD University of Florida College of Law. Civil engineer / attorney by training. FL State Senate 1992-2002 (Senate Minority Leader 1998-2000); 2002 Democratic nominee for FL Attorney General (lost to Charlie Crist). Mayor of Orlando since March 2003 — 48th and longest-serving mayor. Divorced Karen Caudill 2017 (2 sons); remarried Susie Pinson Galloway Shields 2021. 2005 absentee-ballot fraud charge dismissed; reinstated by Gov. Bush. Central public figure in 2016 Pulse nightclub aftermath.

buddy-dyer.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Mayor of Orlando as of June 2026 (orlando.gov bio + Nov 2023 7th-term result + ongoing 2025-26 mayoral statements). 23 years on the job — extensive documented record across the rubric. Sweep of orlando.gov, Orlando Sentinel, Orlando Weekly, ClickOrlando WKMG, WFTV, WESH, Spectrum News 13, CFPublic, WUSF, Florida Politics, Advocate, CNN, NBC News, Tampa Bay Times, Mayors Against Illegal Guns roster, Frost.house.gov, Demings.house.gov, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence — 2 TRUE / 9 FALSE. TRUE: economic_stewardship q2 (12 consecutive years flat millage at 6.65 mills incl. FY26 budget per CFPublic 7/15/2025 — documented restraint posture, though rollback-vs-nominal analysis not surfaced this batch); public_justice q0 (affirmative OPD funding advocacy — publicly thanked Rep. Demings for $1.2M OPD body-cam grant; FY26 budget added 20 OPD employees; routinely appears alongside OPD Chief Eric Smith at briefings — cross-direction for a long-serving urban D). FALSE: biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (served as Grand Marshal of 2016 Come Out With Pride parade; defended FDOT's removal of rainbow Pulse crosswalk as 'cruel political act' in Aug 2025; repeated 'welcoming for LGBTQ+ residents' framing through 2025 Pulse-9 anniversary statement); christian_liberty q4 (under Dyer Orlando flies Pride flag + hosts Pride proclamations / events); public_justice q3 (joined Rep. Maxwell Frost at Office of Gun Violence Prevention presser framing violence as public-health crisis); refuse_state_overreach q2 (declared early COVID state of emergency March 2020; co-led Orange Co mask mandate that persisted past DeSantis Phase 3 reopening); border_immigration q1 + q2 (2018 Orlando 'Trust Act' bars OPD from immigration inquiries — sanctuary-style policy; April 2026 City Council 'unequivocally' opposed local ICE facility); self_defense q0 + q1 (Mayors Against Illegal Guns member + signed 2020 FL mayors letter urging universal background checks + publicly called for ban on 'assault weapons, large capacity magazines and bump stocks' alongside Frost). NULL HOLDS (downgraded from agent's call per skill discipline): sanctity_of_life q1 NULL (agent suggested FALSE on party-pattern inference but no direct quote citing abortion-abolition opposition — kept honest); public_justice q1 NULL (silence on SA-9 Worrell ≠ documented contrary position); refuse_state_overreach q0 NULL (rubric specifies 'UNCONSTITUTIONAL state mandates' — Dyer's resistance is to constitutional state law on sanctuary + Pride crosswalk, opposite cultural direction from rubric intent). After AG Uthmeier removal threat April 2025, Dyer signaled compliance with state immigration law + entered 287(g) training — i.e., backed down rather than refused enforcement (refuse_state_overreach q4 NULL).

0.note: Repeated public commitment to keep Orlando 'welcoming for LGBTQ+ residents' through 2025 Pulse-9 anniversary statement — direct contradiction of one-man-one-woman position

2.note: Pro-LGBTQ+ public posture throughout 23-year tenure; defended rainbow Pulse crosswalk against FDOT removal — no statement affirming biological-sex immutability

4.note: Served as Grand Marshal of 2016 Come Out With Pride parade; defended FDOT's removal of rainbow Pulse-memorial crosswalk as 'cruel political act' (Aug 2025) — sustained LGBTQ-promotion record

Under Dyer, City of Orlando flies Pride flag + hosts Pride proclamations and events; defended rainbow crosswalk — state-funded promotion of non-Christian / counter-Christian symbolism in public institutions

2.note: Held millage rate flat at 6.65 mills for 12 consecutive years incl. FY26 budget — documented restraint posture (rollback-vs-nominal analysis not surfaced this batch; nominal-flat against rising property values is partial restraint, but 12-year discipline is real)

0.note: Publicly thanked Rep. Demings for $1.2M OPD body-cam funding; FY26 budget added 20 OPD employees; routinely appears alongside OPD Chief Eric Smith at briefings — affirmative pro-police advocacy, cross-direction for an urban D

3.note: Joined Rep. Maxwell Frost at federal Office of Gun Violence Prevention press conference framing gun violence as a public-health crisis; $1.5M Community Violence Intervention grant accepted

2.note: Declared early COVID state of emergency March 2020; co-led Orange Co mask mandate that persisted past DeSantis Phase 3 reopening — opposite of protecting citizens from lockdowns/mandates

1.note: Orlando 'Trust Act' (2018) bars OPD from immigration inquiries; April 2026 City Council 'unequivocally' opposed local ICE facility — opposite of mandatory deportation

2.note: Orlando's Trust Act is sanctuary-style policy; Dyer defended it as lawful until AG Uthmeier removal threat April 2025 — pro-sanctuary, anti-federal-preemption

0.note: Member of Mayors Against Illegal Guns / Everytown coalition; signed Feb 2020 FL mayors' letter urging legislature to pass universal background checks — incompatible with constitutional-carry framework

1.note: Publicly called for 'ban on assault weapons, large capacity magazines and bump stocks' alongside Rep. Frost's Office of Gun Violence Prevention launch

set.office: City Commissioner, District 4, City of Orlando, Florida (sitting since 2000 · longest-serving elected official in Orlando history · re-elected Nov 2023 with 64.3% vs Koch and Ross · current term to Jan 10, 2028 · nonpartisan seat but longtime Democrat)

profile.religion: No public faith profession located; narrative is one of departure from religious-conservative upbringing (Orlando Weekly ex-gay-ministry interview)
profile.background: Patty Sheehan, b. ~1962. BA in art from University of Central Florida; prior career as administrator at Florida Dept. of Agriculture and Consumer Services. Elected 2000 — first openly gay elected official in Central Florida. Has spoken publicly about surviving an Orlando-based 'ex-gay' conversion ministry (Eleutheros) at age 18 — defining personal narrative that informs her LGBTQ advocacy. Drove Orlando's Domestic Partner Registry and LGBTQ employment nondiscrimination ordinance. Central public figure in 2016 Pulse nightclub aftermath (the club sits in her district); co-led the OPD Safe Place Initiative with then-Chief John Mina. Hosted a drag queen (Darcel Stevens) at her 2023 victory party in open defiance of Florida's drag-restriction law. HRC / Equality Florida / Victory Fund-aligned throughout 25+ year tenure.

patty-sheehan.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Orlando D4 Commissioner as of June 2026 (orlando.gov bio + Nov 2023 re-election + April 2026 ICE-facility deliberations). 25+ years in seat — extensive documented progressive-LGBTQ-advocate record. Sweep of orlando.gov, Orlando Sentinel, Orlando Weekly, ClickOrlando, WFTV, WESH, Spectrum News 13, CFPublic, WUSF, Florida Politics, CNN, Advocate, LGBTQ Nation, Watermark, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence — 1 TRUE / 9 FALSE. TRUE: refuse_state_overreach q3 (explicit subsidiarity language re: ICE-facility resolution — 'Maybe we'll look at the resolution Orange County did, see if we can pass it at the city level' — content-neutral subsidiarity principle affirmed; scored TRUE under the rubric as written despite leftward direction). FALSE: sanctity_of_life q0 + q1 (endorsed Anna Eskamani — former Planned Parenthood director and lead pro-choice voice in FL Legislature; spoke at Orlando Women's March supporting reproductive rights); biblical_marriage q0 (openly lesbian + drove Orlando Domestic Partner Registry); biblical_marriage q2 (hosted drag-queen Darcel Stevens at her Nov 2023 victory party in open defiance of FL drag-restriction law); biblical_marriage q4 (called FDOT's removal of rainbow Pulse crosswalk a 'slap in the face' + led city's exception request to restore it; Pride proclamations are her signature work); border_immigration q1 + q2 (April 2026 'unequivocally' against local ICE facility; floated resolution opposing local ICE cooperation; suggested legal action to back out of OPD's ICE deal); self_defense q0 + q1 (publicly supports AWB — direct quote 'I am completely and totally frustrated by this nonsense of these assault rifles ... they do not belong on our city streets,' ClickOrlando 2016 + MyNews13 — incompatible with constitutional-carry framework). NULL HOLDS (downgraded from agent's call per skill discipline): family_child_sovereignty q1 + q2 NULL (Eskamani endorsement is HARD on the cells that align with Eskamani's own platform but does not transitively become a direct Sheehan stance on parental-notification / K-12 SOGI — per skill 'Only score a cell when the candidate made a CLEAR, citable statement'); economic_stewardship q2 NULL (agent cited '2015 millage increase + ballotpedia' without verifiable URL; only the same flat-millage CFPublic article was substantively citable — see Dyer for the documented restraint side; mixed/un-verifiable evidence kept NULL honest); public_justice q3 NULL (AWB advocacy ≠ rubric's specific 'public-health excuse to AVOID punishment'; she supports prevention, no documented anti-punishment frame); refuse_state_overreach q0 NULL (rubric specifies 'UNCONSTITUTIONAL state mandates' — her resistance to FDOT crosswalk removal + state immigration preemption is resistance to CONSTITUTIONAL state directives in opposite cultural direction). public_justice q0 NULL — Safe Place Initiative work and police-liaison defense are concrete pro-OPD acts but on specific programs only; doesn't clear 'affirmative back-the-blue advocacy' high bar (she also pushed 2020 racial-equality reforms simultaneously). DEVIATION FLAG: hostile-reader expects 0/100; subsidiarity TRUE on q3 is the honest leftward-direction credit per skill 'documented credit where it is earned' principle.

0.note: Endorsed Anna Eskamani — former Planned Parenthood director and lead pro-choice voice in FL Legislature; spoke at Orlando Women's March supporting reproductive rights — affirmative pro-choice coalition alignment contrary to personhood-at-conception

1.note: Spoke at Orlando Women's March supporting reproductive rights; opposes abortion restrictions — opposite of abolition advocacy

0.note: Openly lesbian; drove Orlando Domestic Partner Registry — affirmative alternative-family-structure recognition contrary to one-man-one-woman

2.note: Openly defied FL drag-restriction law by hosting drag performer Darcel Stevens at Nov 2023 victory party — affirms full LGBTQ ideology including T

4.note: Called FDOT's removal of rainbow Pulse crosswalk a 'slap in the face'; led city's exception request to restore it; Pride proclamations are her signature work — sustained LGBTQ-promotion record

3.note: On ICE-facility resolution: 'Maybe we'll look at the resolution Orange County did, see if we can pass it at the city level' — explicit subsidiarity language (action at lowest competent level); content-neutral principle affirmed, scored TRUE per skill 'documented credit where it is earned' despite leftward direction

1.note: Openly opposes ICE detention facility; allied with immigration advocates; endorsed Eskamani's pro-immigrant platform — opposite of mandatory deportation

2.note: 'Unequivocally' against ICE facility April 2026; floated resolution opposing local ICE cooperation; suggested legal action to back out of OPD's ICE deal — anti-federal-preemption on immigration

0.note: While she owns a firearm, publicly supports restrictive gun policy — incompatible with constitutional-carry framework

1.note: Direct quote: 'I am completely and totally frustrated by this nonsense of these assault rifles ... They do not belong on our city streets' — direct AWB support, incompatible with rubric's opposition to AWB / mag limits / registries
